title: aragallus deflexus
intro: Aragallus deflexus, also known as American water-ginger or wild ginger, is a perennial plant native to North America that grows in wetlands and along streams. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aragallus deflexus include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. Aragallus deflexus can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the roots being the most commonly used part for herbal preparations.
